title: ilex aquifolium subsp. caspia
intro: Ilex aquifolium subsp. caspia, also known as the Caucasian holly or Caspian holly, is a subspecies of the holly plant, characterized by its dark green leaves and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation. The health benefits of Ilex aquifolium subsp. caspia include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
